message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: verdict_from_pvalue significance boundary (p_value == alpha) is untested
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: tests/test_confirm.py 58-102
DESCRIPTION: test_verdict_mapping_covers_three_outcomes only uses clearly-significant (p=0.01 vs alpha=0.05) and clearly-non-significant (p=0.4) inputs, so the inclusive boundary in `if p_value <= alpha` (confirm.py:130) is never pinned. VERIFIED non-discriminating: flipping `p_value <= alpha` to `p_value < alpha` (making a p-value exactly equal to alpha NOT significant — a real change to the decision rule at the threshold) passes the entire confirm + preregistration suite. The same untested boundary applies to the empirical-p comparisons in confirm_whole_corpus (p_contra <= alpha / p_support <= alpha).
SUGGESTED FIX: Add a case with p_value exactly == alpha (e.g. p=0.05, alpha=0.05) asserting SUPPORTED (inclusive boundary), and a whole-corpus case where the empirical p lands exactly on alpha, to lock the documented '<= alpha' rule.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Confirmatory machinery: locking gate, three-verdict mapping, direction-aware null test,
2	input validation, and the unit-level path (audit pass 1: #2, #4, #5, #17)."""
3	
4	import pytest
5	
6	from assay_engine.methodology.confirm import (
7	    confirm_unit_level,
8	    confirm_whole_corpus,
9	    require_locked,
10	    verdict_from_pvalue,
11	)
12	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
13	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import (
14	    Hypothesis,
15	    HypothesisKind,
16	    HypothesisOrigin,
17	)
18	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import VerdictLabel
19	
20	
21	def _locked(kind: HypothesisKind, hid: str = "h1", predicted_direction=None) -> Hypothesis:
22	    return Hypothesis(
23	        hypothesis_id=hid,
24	        statement="x",
25	        kind=kind,
26	        origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
27	        test_name="t",
28	        decision_rule="r",
29	        locked_at="2026-06-15T00:00:00Z",
30	        timestamp_proof="rfc3161:deadbeef",
31	        predicted_direction=predicted_direction,
32	    )
33	
34	
35	def _unlocked(kind: HypothesisKind) -> Hypothesis:
36	    return Hypothesis(
37	        hypothesis_id="h0",
38	        statement="x",
39	        kind=kind,
40	        origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
41	        test_name="t",
42	        decision_rule="r",
43	    )
44	
45	
46	# ---- locking gate ----
47	
48	
49	def test_require_locked_blocks_unlocked_hypothesis():
50	    assert not _unlocked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS).locked
51	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
52	        require_locked(_unlocked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS))
53	
54	
55	# ---- verdict_from_pvalue: three outcomes + validation ----
56	
57	
58	def test_verdict_mapping_covers_three_outcomes():
59	    assert (
60	        verdict_from_pvalue(
61	            "h",
62	            statistic=3.0,
63	            p_value=0.01,
64	            alpha=0.05,
65	            powered=True,
66	            direction_supports_claim=True,
67	        ).label
68	        is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
69	    )
70	    assert (
71	        verdict_from_pvalue(
72	            "h",
73	            statistic=3.0,
74	            p_value=0.01,
75	            alpha=0.05,
76	            powered=True,
77	            direction_supports_claim=False,
78	        ).label
79	        is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
80	    )
81	    assert (
82	        verdict_from_pvalue(
83	            "h",
84	            statistic=0.1,
85	            p_value=0.4,
86	            alpha=0.05,
87	            powered=False,
88	            direction_supports_claim=True,
89	        ).label
90	        is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
91	    )
92	    assert (
93	        verdict_from_pvalue(
94	            "h",
95	            statistic=0.1,
96	            p_value=0.4,
97	            alpha=0.05,
98	            powered=True,
99	            direction_supports_claim=True,
100	        ).label
101	        is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
102	    )
103	
104	
105\[REDACTED](
106	    "kw",
107	    [
108	        {"p_value": -0.1},
109	        {"p_value": 1.5},
110	        {"alpha": 0.0},
111	        {"alpha": 0.5},
112	        {"alpha": 1.0},
113	        {"statistic": float("nan")},
114	        {"statistic": float("inf")},
115	    ],
116	)
117	def test_verdict_from_pvalue_validates_inputs(kw):
118	    base = dict(
119	        statistic=1.0, p_value=0.01, alpha=0.05, powered=True, direction_supports_claim=True
120	    )
121	    base.update(kw)
122	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
123	        verdict_from_pvalue("h", **base)
124	
125	
126	def test_rule_string_is_not_hardcoded_two_sided():
127	    v = verdict_from_pvalue(
128	        "h",
129	        statistic=1.0,
130	        p_value=0.01,
131	        alpha=0.05,
132	        powered=True,
133	        direction_supports_claim=True,
134	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
135	    )
136	    assert "two-sided" not in v.decision_rule
137	    assert "held-out unit-level test" in v.decision_rule
138	
139	
140	# ---- confirm_whole_corpus: direction-aware (#2) + engine-measured stability (#21) ----
141	
142	_STABLE_HI = [100.0] * 20  # resamples far above any small null -> stable for "greater"
143	_STABLE_LO = [-100.0] * 20  # far below -> stable for "less"
144	
145	
146	def test_whole_corpus_supported_upper_tail():
147	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
148	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
149	        observed=10.0,
150	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
151	        alpha=0.05,
152	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
153	        predicted_direction="greater",
154	    )
155	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
156	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 1.0  # engine-measured, not asserted (#21)
157	
158	
159	def test_whole_corpus_supported_lower_tail():
160	    # A claim predicting a LOW statistic, observed far below the null, must be SUPPORTED
161	    # (previously returned INDETERMINATE under direction-blind upper-tail-only logic).
162	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
163	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
164	        observed=0.0,
165	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
166	        alpha=0.05,
167	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
168	        predicted_direction="less",
169	    )
170	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
171	
172	
173	def test_whole_corpus_contradicted_when_significant_in_opposite_tail():
174	    # Claim predicts HIGH, but observed is far below the null -> a real contradiction
175	    # (reportable without resamples; a contradiction does not need stability).
176	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
177	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
178	        observed=0.0,
179	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
180	        alpha=0.05,
181	        predicted_direction="greater",
182	    )
183	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
184	
185	
186	def test_whole_corpus_stability_not_assessed_without_resamples():
187	    # issue #21: SUPPORTED is unavailable if stability was never measured.
188	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
189	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
190	        observed=10.0,
191	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
192	        alpha=0.05,
193	        predicted_direction="greater",
194	    )
195	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
196	    assert v.evidence["stability"] is None
197	
198	
199	def test_whole_corpus_unstable_resamples_is_indeterminate_even_if_beats_null():
200	    # issue #21: resamples that don't reproduce the direction -> not stable -> indeterminate.
201	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
202	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
203	        observed=10.0,
204	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
205	        alpha=0.05,
206	        resample_statistics=[-1.0] * 20,
207	        predicted_direction="greater",
208	    )
209	    assert v.is_indeterminate
210	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 0.0
211	
212	
213	def test_whole_corpus_not_significant_is_indeterminate():
214	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
215	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
216	        observed=5.0,
217	        null_distribution=list(range(100)),
218	        alpha=0.01,
219	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
220	        predicted_direction="greater",
221	    )
222	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
223	
224	
225	def test_whole_corpus_uses_pre_registered_direction():
226	    # issue #24: direction fixed on the locked hypothesis; no confirm-time arg needed.
227	    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="less")
228	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
229	        h,
230	        observed=0.0,
231	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
232	        alpha=0.05,
233	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
234	    )
235	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
236	
237	
238	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_direction_conflict_with_lock():
239	    # issue #24: a confirm-time direction contradicting the pre-registered one is refused.
240	    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="less")
241	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
242	        confirm_whole_corpus(
243	            h,
244	            observed=0.0,
245	            null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
246	            alpha=0.05,
247	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
248	            predicted_direction="greater",
249	        )
250	
251	
252	def test_whole_corpus_requires_some_direction():
253	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
254	        confirm_whole_corpus(
255	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
256	            observed=0.0,
257	            null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
258	            alpha=0.05,
259	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
260	        )
261	
262	
263	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_bad_stability_threshold():
264	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
265	        confirm_whole_corpus(
266	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
267	            observed=1.0,
268	            null_distribution=[0.0],
269	            alpha=0.05,
270	            predicted_direction="greater",
271	            stability_threshold=1.5,
272	        )
273	
274	
275	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_empty_resamples():
276	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
277	        confirm_whole_corpus(
278	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
279	            observed=1.0,
280	            null_distribution=[0.0],
281	            alpha=0.05,
282	            predicted_direction="greater",
283	            resample_statistics=[],
284	        )
285	
286	
287	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_nonfinite_resample():
288	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
289	        confirm_whole_corpus(
290	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
291	            observed=1.0,
292	            null_distribution=[0.0],
293	            alpha=0.05,
294	            predicted_direction="greater",
295	            resample_statistics=[float("nan")],
296	        )
297	
298	
299	def test_whole_corpus_requires_null_distribution():
300	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
301	        confirm_whole_corpus(
302	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
303	            observed=1.0,
304	            null_distribution=[],
305	            alpha=0.05,
306	            predicted_direction="greater",
307	        )
308	
309	
310	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_non_finite():
311	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
312	        confirm_whole_corpus(
313	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
314	            observed=float("nan"),
315	            null_distribution=[1.0, 2.0],
316	            alpha=0.05,
317	            predicted_direction="greater",
318	        )
319	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
320	        confirm_whole_corpus(
321	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
322	            observed=1.0,
323	            null_distribution=[1.0, float("inf")],
324	            alpha=0.05,
325	            predicted_direction="greater",
326	        )
327	
328	
329	def test_whole_corpus_wrong_kind_rejected():
330	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
331	        confirm_whole_corpus(
332	            _locked(HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL),
333	            observed=1.0,
334	            null_distribution=[0.0],
335	            alpha=0.05,
336	            predicted_direction="greater",
337	        )
338	
339	
340	def test_whole_corpus_unlocked_rejected():
341	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
342	        confirm_whole_corpus(
343	            _unlocked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
344	            observed=1.0,
345	            null_distribution=[0.0],
346	            alpha=0.05,
347	            predicted_direction="greater",
348	        )
349	
350	
351	# ---- confirm_unit_level (issue #5, #17) ----
352	
353	
354	def _split() -> DiscoverConfirmSplit:
355	    return DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a", "b"}, {"c", "d"})
356	
357	
358	def test_unit_level_clean_verdict():
359	    v = confirm_unit_level(
360	        _locked(HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL),
361	        split=_split(),
362	        evaluated_ids={"c", "d"},
363	        statistic=2.0,
364	        p_value=0.01,
365	        alpha=0.05,
366	        powered=True,
367	        direction_supports_claim=True,
368	    )
369	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
370	    # issue #20: the deciding direction flag is recorded so the verdict is re-derivable
371	    assert v.evidence["direction_supports_claim"] is True
372	
373	
374	def test_unit_level_wrong_kind_rejected():
375	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
376	        confirm_unit_level(
377	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
378	            split=_split(),
379	            evaluated_ids={"c"},
380	            statistic=2.0,
381	            p_value=0.01,
382	            alpha=0.05,
383	            powered=True,
384	            direction_supports_claim=True,
385	        )
386	
387	
388	def test_unit_level_unlocked_rejected():
389	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
390	        confirm_unit_level(
391	            _unlocked(HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL),
392	            split=_split(),
393	            evaluated_ids={"c"},
394	            statistic=2.0,
395	            p_value=0.01,
396	            alpha=0.05,
397	            powered=True,
398	            direction_supports_claim=True,
399	        )
400	
401	
402	def test_unit_level_rejects_discovery_id_leak():
403	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
404	        confirm_unit_level(
405	            _locked(HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL),
406	            split=_split(),
407	            evaluated_ids={"a"},

413	        )
414	
415

content.content:
1	"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
4	mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:
5	
6	- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
7	  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
8	- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
9	  require stability across resamples.
10	
11	This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
12	are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
13	refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
14	statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
15	``indeterminate``).
16	
17	Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
18	particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
19	claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
20	opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
21	upper-tail-only implementation.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import datetime as _dt
27	import math
28	from statistics import median
29	from typing import Iterable, Sequence
30	
31	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
32	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
33	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
34	    TimestampAuthority,
35	    require_preregistered,
36	)
37	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
38	
39	
40	def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
41	    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.
42	
43	    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
44	    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
45	    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
46	    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
47	    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
48	    """
49	    if authority is not None:
50	        require_preregistered(
51	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
52	        )
53	    else:
54	        require_locked(hypothesis)
55	
56	
57	def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
58	    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.
59	
60	    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
61	    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
62	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
63	    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
64	    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
65	    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
66	    """
67	    if not hypothesis.locked:
68	        raise FirewallViolation(
69	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
70	            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
71	        )
72	
73	
74	def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
75	    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
76	    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
77	    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
78	    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
79	        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")
80	
81	
82	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
83	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
84	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
85	
86	
87	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
88	    if not math.isfinite(value):
89	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
90	
91	
92	def verdict_from_pvalue(
93	    hypothesis_id: str,
94	    *,
95	    statistic: float,
96	    p_value: float,
97	    alpha: float,
98	    powered: bool,
99	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
100	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
101	) -> Verdict:
102	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
103	
104	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
105	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
106	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
107	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
108	
109	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
110	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
111	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
112	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
113	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
114	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
115	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
116	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
117	    """
118	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
119	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
120	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
121	
122	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
123	    common = dict(
124	        statistic=statistic,
125	        threshold=alpha,
126	        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
127	    )
128	    if not powered:
129	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
130	    if p_value <= alpha:
131	        if direction_supports_claim:
132	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
133	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
134	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
135	
136	
137	def confirm_unit_level(
138	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
139	    *,
140	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
141	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
142	    statistic: float,
143	    p_value: float,
144	    alpha: float,
145	    powered: bool,
146	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
147	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
148	) -> Verdict:
149	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
150	
151	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
152	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
153	    """
154	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
155	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
156	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
157	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
158	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
159	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
160	        statistic=statistic,
161	        p_value=p_value,
162	        alpha=alpha,
163	        powered=powered,
164	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
165	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
166	    )
167	
168	
169	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
170	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
171	    n = len(null)
172	    if tail == "greater":
173	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
174	    else:
175	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
176	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
177	
178	
179	def _resample_stability(resamples: Sequence[float], reference: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
180	    """Fraction of resampled statistics that fall on the predicted side of ``reference``.
181	
182	    A measured stand-in for "the effect direction reproduces across resamples": the closer
183	    to 1.0, the more consistently the resampled statistic stays in the predicted tail.
184	    """
185	    if tail == "greater":
186	        agree = sum(1 for r in resamples if r > reference)
187	    else:
188	        agree = sum(1 for r in resamples if r < reference)
189	    return agree / len(resamples)
190	
191	
192	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
193	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time
194	    argument is optional and, if given, must match the pre-registered one."""
195	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
196	    if locked_dir is not None:
197	        if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
198	            raise ValueError(
199	                "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
200	                f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
201	            )
202	        return locked_dir
203	    if predicted_direction is None:
204	        raise ValueError(
205	            "no predicted_direction: set it on the hypothesis at pre-registration "
206	            "(preferred) or pass it explicitly"
207	        )
208	    return predicted_direction
209	
210	
211	def confirm_whole_corpus(
212	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
213	    *,
214	    observed: float,
215	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
216	    alpha: float,
217	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
218	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
219	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
220	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
221	) -> Verdict:
222	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
223	
224	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
225	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
226	
227	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
228	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
229	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
230	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
231	
232	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
233	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
234	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
235	
236	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
237	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
238	    """
239	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
240	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
241	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
242	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
243	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
244	    if not null_distribution:
245	        raise ValueError(
246	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
247	        )
248	    for v in null_distribution:
249	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
250	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
251	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
252	
253	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
254	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
255	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
256	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
257	    n = len(null_distribution)
258	
259	    stability: float | None = None
260	    if resample_statistics is not None:
261	        if not resample_statistics:
262	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
263	        for r in resample_statistics:
264	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
265	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, median(null_distribution), direction)
266	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
267	
268	    rule = (
269	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
270	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
271	    )
272	    evidence = {
273	        "p_support": p_support,
274	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
275	        "null_n": n,
276	        "predicted_direction": direction,
277	        "stability": stability,
278	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
279	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
280	    }
281	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
282	
283	    # A contradiction (significant in the opposite tail) is reportable without stability.
284	    if p_contra <= alpha:
285	        return Verdict.contradicted(
286	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
287	        )
288	    if stability is None:
289	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
290	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
291	            rule,
292	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
293	            **common,
294	        )
295	    if not stable:
296	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
297	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
298	        )
299	    if p_support <= alpha:
300	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
301	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
302	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
303	    )
304

content.text:
Restored CLEAN. (The earlier `DIRTY` was a stale stat cache; `git diff` shows no content delta, and a `git checkout` confirms clean.)

Verification complete. The finding is TRUE and accurately reported.

Findings:
- Decision rule in code uses inclusive boundaries: `confirm.py:130` (`if p_value <= alpha`), `confirm.py:284` (`if p_contra <= alpha`), `confirm.py:299` (`if p_support <= alpha`). The docstring at lines 102-116 documents this as the intended contract.
- The existing `test_verdict_mapping_covers_three_outcomes` (test_confirm.py:58-102) only uses p=0.01 (clearly significant) and p=0.4 (clearly non-significant) against alpha=0.05; no whole-corpus test produces an empirical p exactly equal to alpha either.
- PoC mutation: flipping all three `<= alpha` to `< alpha` (a real change to the threshold decision rule — p exactly at alpha would flip SUPPORTED/CONTRADICTED to INDETERMINATE) passes test_confirm.py (31/31), the confirm+prereg filter (61/61), AND the full suite (407 passed, 5 skipped). The boundary is genuinely unguarded by any test.

Severity calibration: LOW is correct, and I'd argue it's at the low end of LOW. This is a test-quality gap (a missing mutation-killing case), not a behavioral defect — the production code is correct and matches its documented contract. The practical exposure is minimal: an empirical p-value can land exactly on alpha (e.g. the +1-smoothed `_empirical_p` yields rational values that can equal a rational alpha, and unit-level callers can pass any p), but a regression to the boundary would be an edge-of-threshold misclassification, not a systemic failure. The assessor's suggested fix (add a p==alpha SUPPORTED case and a whole-corpus case landing exactly on alpha) is the right, minimal remediation. No regression introduced by pass-1 here; this gap is pre-existing.

input.reasoning: TRUE finding, accurately characterized. The decision rule in confirm.py is inclusive at the threshold: line 130 `if p_value <= alpha`, line 284 `if p_contra <= alpha`, line 299 `if p_support <= alpha`, and the docstring (lines 102-116) documents this as the intended contract. The test suite never exercises p == alpha: test_verdict_mapping_covers_three_outcomes (test_confirm.py:58-102) uses only p=0.01 (clearly significant) and p=0.4 (clearly non-significant) vs alpha=0.05, and no whole-corpus test produces an empirical p that equals alpha. I confirmed the boundary is non-discriminating via mutation testing: replacing all three `<= alpha` with `< alpha` (a genuine change to the at-threshold decision — p exactly at alpha would flip SUPPORTED/CONTRADICTED to INDETERMINATE) leaves the suite fully green at every scope. This is a test-quality gap, not a behavioral defect: the production code is correct and matches its documented '<= alpha' contract; nothing is broken at runtime. Severity is correctly LOW (low end of LOW): the only exposure is that a future regression flipping the boundary operator would ship undetected, causing edge-of-threshold misclassification of cases where the empirical/supplied p lands exactly on alpha (reachable, since +1-smoothed _empirical_p yields rationals and unit-level p is caller-supplied). Not a pass-1 regression; pre-existing gap. The assessor's suggested fix (add a p==alpha SUPPORTED case plus a whole-corpus case landing exactly on alpha) is the correct minimal remediation.

Tests with only clear-cut inputs: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_confirm.py:58-102 (p=0.01 and p=0.4 vs alpha=0.05). PoC mutation (operators flipped to `<`): baseline tests/test_confirm.py = 31 passed; after mutation tests/test_confirm.py = 31 passed (unchanged); confirm+prereg filter = 61 passed; FULL suite = 407 passed, 5 skipped — the threshold-boundary change kills zero tests, proving p==alpha is untested.
